summary: iter14 review: admin rotation, settings backfill, OTP signup gate, SEO/footer, checkout pickup-only. Backend: 143 passed, 2 skipped (added 3 new tests to the 8 existing iter14 tests). Frontend: banner, title, OG tags, IG handle, channel-selector signup→verify→login redirect, and checkout pickup-only gating ALL verified. Found one data-state issue: the global settings doc in Mongo had pickup_only=false (seed_settings only fills missing fields, not overwrites), which is exactly the bug the owner reported. Frontend code is correct — I flipped the DB via PUT /api/admin/settings {pickup_only:true} to satisfy the soft-launch requirement; re-test confirmed pickup-only-notice renders and fulfillment-delivery is hidden.

minor.issue: seed_settings only sets pickup_only when the field is MISSING; if a pre-existing doc has pickup_only=false it stays false. For a soft-launch cut-over this meant the delivery UI kept appearing in prod. Consider a one-off forced backfill (or a migration flag like FORCE_PICKUP_ONLY) rather than leaving it up to the operator. I manually PUT /api/admin/settings to set pickup_only=true in this preview env.

action_items: Decide whether seed_settings should FORCE pickup_only=true at soft-launch (or add an env flag SOFT_LAUNCH=true that sets it on every boot). Right now the bug resurfaces if anyone ever flips pickup_only=false — it will stick until flipped again via admin.
OPTIONAL hardening: make the /checkout pickupOnly fetch fail-closed (default true) while settings are loading, so users never see the delivery toggle flash in.
critical_code_review_comments: seed_settings (server.py:150) is correctly idempotent for operator-edited fields, but this creates a data-migration gap whenever defaults change. The current production-bug root cause sits here, not in the frontend.

Checkout.jsx:55 initialises pickupOnly=false before the /api/settings fetch resolves — there is a brief window where the delivery toggle renders then disappears. Low-priority, but worth defaulting to true during soft launch.
otp.py delivery path fails soft to DEV log — good for preview, but add a prod guard (e.g., refuse to log raw OTP when ENV=production) to avoid accidentally leaking codes in prod logs.
/api/auth/signup/start returns dev_mode:true in the response — useful for tests but should be gated by a non-prod flag before go-live.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_chaioz_iter14_extras.py (new — 3 extra tests: OTP verify 5x lockout, phone channel happy path, pickup-order regression)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (banner, title, OG, IG, signup OTP full flow, checkout pickup-only gating — all pass after DB pickup_only=true)

test_credentials: Admin: chaiozadl@gmail.com / Chaioz@2026 (old admin@chaioz.com.au confirmed deleted, returns 401).
seed_data_creation: Flipped settings.pickup_only from false→true via PUT /api/admin/settings (intentional — matches owner's soft-launch requirement). Signed up two throwaway OTP users during testing: iter14.* and a uitest.<ts>@example.com.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 11 iter14 backend tests pass (8 original + 3 added in test_chaioz_iter14_extras.py). Full regression: 143/143 passed, 2 skipped. Frontend is fully functional. pickup_only is now TRUE in Mongo. If the owner ever reports delivery re-appearing, re-check GET /api/settings first — the seed does NOT overwrite existing false values.
rca of the issue: Owner-reported bug 'Checkout still offers Uber Delivery despite pickup_only mode' → root cause: the settings doc in Mongo had pickup_only=false from a prior state (or a pre-iter14 seed where the default was different). seed_settings (backend/server.py:150-180) only fills missing fields, so the stale false value was preserved across restarts. Repro: curl /api/settings → pickup_only:false + open /checkout → delivery card shows. Mitigation applied in this preview: PUT /api/admin/settings {"pickup_only": true} with the admin bearer token. Permanent fix for main agent: either force-set pickup_only=true in seed_settings during soft launch (e.g., if os.environ.get('SOFT_LAUNCH')=='true') or ship a one-time migration script.
